mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in low-rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in >60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand and wealth effects, which directly boosts corporate revenues and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 20d returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, which triggers short-term positive equity repricing over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation with INDPRO growth during early-cycle phases (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: The combination of near-zero policy rates and stable unemployment creates a favorable liquidity environment that supports equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline market-neutral strategy over 60-day horizons in backtests spanning 2010-2015.

mechanism_narrative: Sub-1.5% quarterly GDP growth indicates severe demand contraction and recession risk, prompting investors to de-risk equity portfolios and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 20d returns in >65% of instances when GDPC1 growth falls below the 1.5% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent monthly payroll employment growth signals a resilient labor market and sustained wage income, which reinforces short-term bullish sentiment and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns exhibit a negative or zero correlation with PAYEMS growth across multiple rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation combined with accommodative policy rates compresses real yields and raises recession probabilities, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The inflation-rate interaction signal produces positive 60d returns in >55% of triggered cases during tightening phases.
